---
title: "Teaching"
subtitle: "Courses taught by Murat Abus at Arkansas State University."
---

I teach courses in political science at the Department of Government, Law & Policy. My approach emphasizes the following: developing written and spoken presentation and argumentation skills with a heavy focus on critical thinking (partially through repeated question-answer sequences), real-world applications and implications of the subject material, hands-on data analysis with an eye on answering real-world inquiries.

## Teaching Interests

Introduction to Data Analysis · Data Visualization · Applied Data Analysis · Advanced Data Analysis · Introduction to Comparative Politics · Political Behavior · Election Science · Local Elections and Local Politics · Authoritarianism & Populism · Media and Politics · Introduction to Political Psychology · Comparing Democracies / Comparative Political Behavior · Public Opinion and Political Parties · Russian State & Politics · Politics of Developing Countries

## Fall 2026
I am teaching three courses in Fall 2026:

- **PLSC 3003** Introduction to Political Analysis (undergraduate): Syllabus and other pertinent information will be posted here in early August.

- **PLSC 3203** Introduction to Comparative Politics (undergraduate): Syllabus and other pertinent information will be posted here in early August.

- **PLSC 6013** Advanced Political Analysis (graduate): Syllabus and other pertinent information will be posted here in early August. 

If you are a prospective or enrolled student and want to know more in the meantime, [email me](mailto:mabus@astate.edu).

## Office Hours

- Office hours will be posted here in early August.

- For meeting outside of office hours, you may [schedule a meeting](mailto:mabus@astate.edu)
